sentences.target: Deine Schrift ist schwer zu lesen.

translations.de: Deine Schrift ist schwer zu lesen.
translations.en: Your handwriting is hard to read.
translations.es: Tu letra es difícil de leer.
translations.fr: Ton écriture est difficile à lire.
translations.it: La tua scrittura è difficile da leggere.
translations.pt: Sua letra é difícil de ler.
translations.nl: Jouw handschrift is moeilijk te lezen.
translations.pl: Twoje pismo jest trudne do odczytania.
translations.el: Ο γραφικός σου χαρακτήρας διαβάζεται δύσκολα.
translations.nb: Håndskriften din er vanskelig å lese.
translations.sv: Din handstil är svår att läsa.
translations.ro: Scrisul tău e greu de citit.
translations.da: Din håndskrift er svær at læse.
translations.ja: あなたの字は読みにくいです。

tokens.explanation: Possessive determiner modifying 'Schrift' (feminine nominative singular), marking 2nd person singular possession.
Head noun; subject in nominative singular feminine; here referring to the addressee's handwriting.
Copula linking the subject 'Deine Schrift' to the predicative complement.
Predicative adjective expressing difficulty.
Infinitival marker introducing the non-finite clause with 'lesen'.
Infinitive verb; forms an infinitival clause with 'zu' complementing 'schwer' ('schwer zu lesen').

sentences.target: Ich ändere die Schrift im Dokument.

translations.de: Ich ändere die Schrift im Dokument.
translations.en: I’m changing the font in the document.
translations.es: Estoy cambiando la fuente en el documento.
translations.fr: Je change la police dans le document.
translations.it: Sto cambiando il carattere nel documento.
translations.yue: 我而家喺度改份文件嘅字體。
translations.pt: Estou mudando a fonte no documento.
translations.vi: Tôi đang đổi phông chữ trong tài liệu.
translations.nl: Ik verander het lettertype in het document.
translations.hi: मैं दस्तावेज़ में फ़ॉन्ट बदल रहा हूँ।
translations.id: Aku sedang mengganti font di dokumen.
translations.ja: 文書のフォントを変えています。

tokens.explanation: 1st person singular personal pronoun; nominative subject.
Finite main verb (present, 1st person singular).
Definite article marking 'Schrift' as a specific referent (accusative singular feminine).
Direct object; here denotes the font/typeface being changed.
Preposition (contraction of 'in dem') governing dative; introduces the PP 'im Dokument' indicating location.
Object of the preposition 'im' (dative context); denotes where the change takes place.

sentences.target: Die Lehrerin lobt meine Schrift heute.

translations.de: Die Lehrerin lobt meine Schrift heute.
translations.en: The teacher is praising my handwriting today.
translations.es: Hoy la profesora está elogiando mi letra.
translations.fr: La prof me félicite pour mon écriture aujourd'hui.
translations.it: Oggi la maestra mi fa i complimenti per la calligrafia.
translations.pt: Hoje a professora está elogiando minha letra.
translations.he: המורה משבחת את הכתב שלי היום.
translations.vi: Hôm nay cô giáo khen chữ viết của tôi.
translations.nl: De leraar prijst vandaag mijn handschrift.
translations.hu: Ma a tanár dicséri a kézírásomat.
translations.hi: आज अध्यापिका मेरी लिखावट की तारीफ कर रही है।
translations.el: Σήμερα η δασκάλα με επαινεί για τα γράμματά μου.
translations.nb: Læreren roser håndskriften min i dag.
translations.id: Hari ini guru memuji tulisan tanganku.
translations.sv: Läraren berömmer min handstil i dag.
translations.da: Læreren roser min håndskrift i dag.
translations.ja: 今日は先生が私の字をほめています。

tokens.explanation: Definite article for 'Lehrerin' (nominative singular feminine).
Head noun; subject (nominative singular), specifically a female teacher.
Finite main verb (present, 3rd person singular).
Possessive determiner modifying 'Schrift' (accusative singular), marking 1st person singular possession.
Direct object receiving the action of 'lobt'; refers to the speaker's handwriting.
Temporal adverb indicating the time of the action.

sentences.target: Auf dem Schild gefällt mir die Schrift.
sentences.reading: /aʊ̯f deːm ʃɪlt ɡəˈfɛlt miːɐ̯ diː ʃrɪft./

translations.de: Auf dem Schild gefällt mir die Schrift.
translations.ipa: /aʊ̯f deːm ʃɪlt ɡəˈfɛlt miːɐ̯ diː ʃrɪft./
translations.en: I like the lettering on the sign.
translations.es: Me gusta la tipografía del letrero.
translations.he: הכיתוב על השלט מוצא חן בעיניי.
translations.vi: Tôi thích kiểu chữ trên biển hiệu.
translations.nl: Ik vind het lettertype op het bord mooi.
translations.hi: मुझे बोर्ड पर की लिखावट पसंद है।
translations.pl: Podoba mi się liternictwo na szyldzie.
translations.el: Μου αρέσουν τα γράμματα στην πινακίδα.
translations.id: Aku suka tulisan pada papan nama itu.
translations.ro: Îmi place scrisul de pe firmă.
translations.cs: Líbí se mi písmo na ceduli.
translations.da: Jeg kan lide skrifttypen på skiltet.
translations.ja: 看板の文字が気に入っています。

tokens.explanation: Preposition governing dative here; introduces the PP 'Auf dem Schild' indicating location.
Definite article in dative singular agreeing with 'Schild'.
Object of the preposition 'auf' (dative); the sign/notice as a location.
Finite main verb (present, 3rd person singular); takes a dative experiencer ('mir') and a nominative theme ('die Schrift').
1st person singular personal pronoun in dative; experiencer of 'gefällt'.
Definite article marking 'Schrift' as the specific theme (nominative singular feminine).
Nominative subject/theme causing the feeling of liking; here the lettering on the sign.
